intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "× Elyleymus adamsii." However, I found information on a similar plant, Elymus × adamsii, which is a hybrid of Elymus caninus and possibly Elymus cristatus. 

If you are referring to this plant, here is a paragraph about Elymus × adamsii:

Elymus × adamsii is a type of grass, a hybrid of the bearded couch grass (Elymus caninus) and the crested hair-grass (Elymus cristatus). The health benefits of Elymus × adamsii are largely anecdotal, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include phenolic compounds, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elymus × adamsii is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, although it is not widely used due to its limited availability and lack of scientific research on its efficacy.
